я.meaning: 1st person singular nominative. I (subject). Я очень счастлив. I am very happy.

меня.meaning: 1st person singular genitive/accusative. Me (direct object or after prepositions). Они видели меня. They saw me.

мне.meaning: 1st person singular dative. To/for me. Он дал мне книгу. He gave me a book.

мной.meaning: 1st person singular instrumental. With/by me. Он пошёл со мной. He went with me.

мною.meaning: 1st person singular instrumental (archaic variant). With/by me.

ты.meaning: 2nd person singular nominative. You (subject, informal). Ты знаешь ответ? Do you know the answer?

тебя.meaning: 2nd person singular genitive/accusative. You (object). Я вижу тебя. I see you.

тебе.meaning: 2nd person singular dative. To/for you (informal). Я дам тебе помощь. I will give you help.

тобой.meaning: 2nd person singular instrumental. With/by you (informal). Я иду с тобой. I'm going with you.

тобою.meaning: 2nd person singular instrumental (archaic variant). With/by you.

он.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular nominative. He/it (subject). Он учитель. He is a teacher.

его.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular genitive/accusative animate. Him (object) or his (possessive after preposition). Я видел его. I saw him.

него.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular genitive (after preposition with n-prefix). Him (after preposition). В нём живёт кот. In him lives a cat (in him = in that place).

ему.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular dative. To/for him. Я дал ему книгу. I gave him a book.

им.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular/plural instrumental. With/by him/them. Я иду с ним. I'm going with him.

нём.meaning: 3rd person masculine singular prepositional (after preposition with n-prefix). In/about/on him. Я говорил о нём. I spoke about him.

она.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular nominative. She/it (subject). Она врач. She is a doctor.

её.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular genitive/accusative animate. Her (object) or her (possessive after preposition). Я видел её. I saw her.

неё.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular genitive (after preposition with n-prefix). Her (after preposition). Я говорил о ней. I spoke about her.

ей.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular dative. To/for her. Я дал ей цветы. I gave her flowers.

ней.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular prepositional (after preposition with n-prefix). In/about/on her. В ней есть ум. In her exists mind (she is intelligent).

оно.meaning: 3rd person neuter singular nominative. It (subject). Это окно очень большое. This window is very big (it = window).

мы.meaning: 1st person plural nominative. We (subject). Мы работаем вместе. We work together.

нас.meaning: 1st person plural genitive/accusative. Us (object or after preposition). Они видят нас. They see us.

нам.meaning: 1st person plural dative. To/for us. Учитель дал нам задание. The teacher gave us an assignment.

нами.meaning: 1st person plural instrumental. With/by us. Я иду с вами. I'm going with you (note: formal вами instead).

вы.meaning: 2nd person plural/formal nominative. You (formal/plural). Вы знаете ответ? Do you (formal) know the answer?

вас.meaning: 2nd person plural/formal genitive/accusative. You (formal/plural object). Я вас вижу. I see you (formal).

вам.meaning: 2nd person plural/formal dative. To/for you (formal). Я вам дам помощь. I will help you (formal).

вами.meaning: 2nd person plural/formal instrumental. With/by you (formal). Я иду с вами. I'm going with you (formal).

они.meaning: 3rd person plural nominative. They (subject). Они студенты. They are students.

их.meaning: 3rd person plural genitive/accusative animate. Them (object) or their (possessive, indeclinable). Я видел их. I saw them.

них.meaning: 3rd person plural genitive (after preposition with n-prefix). Them (after preposition). Я говорил о них. I spoke about them.

им.meaning: 3rd person plural dative. To/for them. Я дал им деньги. I gave them money.

ими.meaning: 3rd person plural instrumental. With/by them. Я иду с ними. I'm going with them.

себя.meaning: Reflexive pronoun (genitive/accusative). Self. Oneself. Он видит себя. He sees himself.

себе.meaning: Reflexive pronoun (dative). To/for oneself. Она купила себе платье. She bought herself a dress.

собой.meaning: Reflexive pronoun (instrumental). With/by oneself. Я беру с собой книгу. I take a book with me.

собою.meaning: Reflexive pronoun (instrumental, archaic variant). With/by oneself.

мой.meaning: 1st person singular masculine nominative possessive. My. Мой дом большой. My house is big.

моя.meaning: 1st person singular feminine nominative possessive. My (feminine). Моя книга интересная. My book is interesting.

моё.meaning: 1st person singular neuter nominative possessive. My (neuter). Моё письмо готово. My letter is ready.

мои.meaning: 1st person singular plural nominative possessive. My (plural). Мои друзья приехали. My friends arrived.

моего.meaning: 1st person singular masculine/neuter genitive possessive. My (genitive). Я не вижу моего портрета. I don't see my portrait.

моей.meaning: 1st person singular feminine genitive possessive. My (feminine genitive). Цвет моей рубашки красный. The color of my shirt is red.

твой.meaning: 2nd person singular masculine nominative possessive. Your (informal). Твой дом далеко? Is your house far?

твоя.meaning: 2nd person singular feminine nominative possessive. Your (informal feminine). Твоя сумка здесь. Your bag is here.

твоё.meaning: 2nd person singular neuter nominative possessive. Your (informal neuter). Твоё мнение важно. Your opinion is important.

твои.meaning: 2nd person singular plural nominative possessive. Your (informal plural). Твои работы хорошие. Your works are good.

его.meaning: 3rd person masculine/neuter singular possessive determiner (indeclinable). His/Its. Это его книга. This is his book.

её.meaning: 3rd person feminine singular possessive determiner (indeclinable). Her. Это её дом. This is her house.

их.meaning: 3rd person plural possessive determiner (indeclinable). Their. Это их квартира. This is their apartment.

наш.meaning: 1st person plural masculine nominative possessive. Our. Наш город красивый. Our city is beautiful.

наша.meaning: 1st person plural feminine nominative possessive. Our (feminine). Наша школа новая. Our school is new.

наше.meaning: 1st person plural neuter nominative possessive. Our (neuter). Наше правительство работает. Our government is working.

наши.meaning: 1st person plural nominative possessive. Our (plural). Наши дети учатся. Our children are studying.

ваш.meaning: 2nd person plural/formal masculine nominative possessive. Your (formal). Ваш дом очень красивый. Your house is very beautiful.

ваша.meaning: 2nd person plural/formal feminine nominative possessive. Your (formal feminine). Ваша работа превосходна. Your work is excellent.

ваше.meaning: 2nd person plural/formal neuter nominative possessive. Your (formal neuter). Ваше мнение интересно. Your opinion is interesting.

ваши.meaning: 2nd person plural/formal nominative possessive. Your (formal plural). Ваши заметки полезны. Your notes are useful.

свой.meaning: Reflexive possessive masculine nominative. One's own. Свой дом самый лучший. One's own house is the best.

своя.meaning: Reflexive possessive feminine nominative. One's own (feminine). Каждый защищает свою семью. Everyone protects their own family.

своё.meaning: Reflexive possessive neuter nominative. One's own (neuter). Я люблю своё дело. I love my own work.

свои.meaning: Reflexive possessive plural nominative. One's own (plural). Люди ценят своих друзей. People value their own friends.

этот.meaning: Demonstrative masculine nominative. This (near). Этот дом большой. This house is big.

эта.meaning: Demonstrative feminine nominative. This (near feminine). Эта книга интересная. This book is interesting.

это.meaning: Demonstrative neuter nominative or pronoun. This (near neuter) or that (general). Это мой дом. This is my house.

эти.meaning: Demonstrative plural nominative. These (near). Эти люди добрые. These people are kind.

тот.meaning: Demonstrative masculine nominative. That (far). Тот дом старый. That house is old.

та.meaning: Demonstrative feminine nominative. That (far feminine). Та женщина красивая. That woman is beautiful.

то.meaning: Demonstrative neuter nominative or pronoun. That (far neuter). То, что ты сказал, верно. What you said is true.

те.meaning: Demonstrative plural nominative. Those (far). Те студенты умные. Those students are smart.

такой.meaning: Demonstrative masculine nominative. Such/of that kind. Такой же дом есть рядом. Such a house is nearby.

такая.meaning: Demonstrative feminine nominative. Such (feminine). Такая красивая девушка! Such a beautiful girl!

такое.meaning: Demonstrative neuter nominative. Such (neuter). Такое не должно быть. Such shouldn't be.

такие.meaning: Demonstrative plural nominative. Such (plural). Такие дни редкие. Such days are rare.

кто.meaning: Interrogative pronoun nominative. Who (person). Кто это? Who is this?

кого.meaning: Interrogative pronoun genitive/accusative animate. Whom (person object). Кого ты видел? Whom did you see?

кому.meaning: Interrogative pronoun dative. To whom. Кому ты дал деньги? To whom did you give money?

кем.meaning: Interrogative pronoun instrumental. With/by whom. Кем она работает? What does she work as?

о_ком.meaning: Interrogative pronoun prepositional. About/of whom (requires о preposition). О ком ты думаешь? About whom are you thinking?

что.meaning: Interrogative pronoun nominative/accusative. What (thing). Что это? What is this?

чего.meaning: Interrogative pronoun genitive. What (thing genitive). Чего ты хочешь? What do you want?

чему.meaning: Interrogative pronoun dative. To what (teaching). Чему ты учишься? What are you studying?

чем.meaning: Interrogative pronoun instrumental. With/by what. Чем ты рисуешь? What are you drawing with?

о_чём.meaning: Interrogative pronoun prepositional. About what. О чём вы говорите? What are you talking about?

какой.meaning: Interrogative adjective masculine nominative. What kind. Какой ты человек? What kind of person are you?

какая.meaning: Interrogative adjective feminine nominative. What kind (feminine). Какая это музыка? What kind of music is this?

какое.meaning: Interrogative adjective neuter nominative. What kind (neuter). Какое сегодня число? What is today's date?

какие.meaning: Interrogative adjective plural nominative. What kind (plural). Какие у тебя планы? What are your plans?

чей.meaning: Interrogative possessive masculine nominative. Whose. Чей это дом? Whose house is this?

чья.meaning: Interrogative possessive feminine nominative. Whose (feminine). Чья это книга? Whose book is this?

чьё.meaning: Interrogative possessive neuter nominative. Whose (neuter). Чьё это письмо? Whose letter is this?

чьи.meaning: Interrogative possessive plural nominative. Whose (plural). Чьи это вещи? Whose things are these?

который.meaning: Relative pronoun masculine nominative. Who/which (agrees with noun). Мальчик, который приехал, мой брат. The boy who arrived is my brother.

которая.meaning: Relative pronoun feminine nominative. Who/which (feminine). Девушка, которая поёт, очень талантлива. The girl who sings is very talented.

которое.meaning: Relative pronoun neuter nominative. Which (neuter). Письмо, которое я написал, потеряно. The letter which I wrote is lost.

которые.meaning: Relative pronoun plural nominative. Which (plural). Друзья, которые приехали, радостны. The friends who arrived are happy.

никто.meaning: Negative pronoun nominative. Nobody/no one. Никто не пришёл. Nobody came.

ничто.meaning: Negative pronoun nominative. Nothing. Ничто его не беспокоит. Nothing bothers him.

ничего.meaning: Negative pronoun genitive. Nothing (genitive). Я ничего не вижу. I see nothing.

не.meaning: Negation particle. Not. Я не знаю. I don't know.

ни.meaning: Negation particle (emphatic). Neither/nor. Ни один человек не пришёл. Not a single person came.

и.meaning: Coordinating conjunction. And. Мама и папа приехали. Mom and dad arrived.

но.meaning: Coordinating conjunction. But. Она умная, но ленивая. She is smart but lazy.

или.meaning: Coordinating conjunction. Or. Чай или кофе? Tea or coffee?

да.meaning: Coordinating conjunction (archaic/poetic). And. Да будет свет! And let there be light!

ни.meaning: Coordinating conjunction (emphatic pairing). Neither...nor. Ни ты, ни я не знаю. Neither you nor I know.

что.meaning: Subordinating conjunction. That (complement clause). Я знаю, что это верно. I know that this is true.

если.meaning: Subordinating conjunction. If (conditional). Если ты придёшь, мы пойдём. If you come, we'll go.

когда.meaning: Subordinating conjunction. When (temporal). Когда ты приедешь? When will you arrive?

потому_что.meaning: Subordinating conjunction. Because (causal). Я устал, потому что много работал. I'm tired because I worked a lot.

пока.meaning: Subordinating conjunction. While/until (temporal). Пока я писал, он читал. While I was writing, he was reading.

хотя.meaning: Subordinating conjunction. Although/though (concessive). Хотя было холодно, мы гуляли. Although it was cold, we walked.

как.meaning: Subordinating conjunction. As/how/when (temporal/manner). Как ты пришёл, я был дома. When you arrived, I was home.

чтобы.meaning: Subordinating conjunction. In order to/so that (purpose). Я учусь, чтобы сдать экзамен. I study so that I pass the exam.

в.meaning: Preposition. In/to (governs acc for motion, prep for location). Я иду в школу. I'm going to school.

на.meaning: Preposition. On/onto/to (governs acc for motion, prep for location). Она положила книгу на стол. She put the book on the table.

из.meaning: Preposition. Out of/from (governs genitive). Я пришёл из школы. I came from school.

к.meaning: Preposition. To/towards (governs dative). Я иду к другу. I'm going to my friend.

с.meaning: Preposition. With/from (governs genitive or instrumental). Я иду с братом. I'm going with my brother.

у.meaning: Preposition. At/near (governs genitive). У меня есть дом. At me is a house (I have a house).

для.meaning: Preposition. For (governs genitive). Это письмо для тебя. This letter is for you.

без.meaning: Preposition. Without (governs genitive). Я пришёл без денег. I came without money.

о.meaning: Preposition. About/of (governs prepositional). Я говорю о России. I'm talking about Russia.

об.meaning: Preposition. About/of (variant before vowel). Он думал об учёбе. He thought about studying.

по.meaning: Preposition. Along/by/per (governs dative). Мы идём по улице. We're walking along the street.

над.meaning: Preposition. Over/above (governs instrumental). Птица летит над домом. The bird flies over the house.

под.meaning: Preposition. Under/below (governs accusative or instrumental). Кот сидит под столом. The cat is sitting under the table.

перед.meaning: Preposition. Before/in front of (governs instrumental). Мальчик стоит перед школой. The boy is standing in front of the school.

за.meaning: Preposition. Behind/beyond (governs accusative or instrumental). Дом стоит за лесом. The house is behind the forest.

между.meaning: Preposition. Between (governs instrumental). Он сидит между ними. He is sitting between them.

около.meaning: Preposition. Near/around (governs genitive). Мой дом около парка. My house is near the park.

во.meaning: Preposition. In/to (variant before consonant clusters). Во время урока я учусь. During the lesson I study.

быть.meaning: Auxiliary/copula verb. To be. Я был там. I was there.

есть.meaning: Present tense of быть (to be, existential). Он есть здесь. He is here. (Also archaic/formal.)

был.meaning: Past masculine singular of быть. Was. Он был учителем. He was a teacher.

была.meaning: Past feminine singular of быть. Was (feminine). Она была студенткой. She was a student.

было.meaning: Past neuter singular of быть. Was (neuter). Это было давно. That was long ago.

были.meaning: Past plural of быть. Were. Мы были в городе. We were in the city.

будет.meaning: Future 3rd person singular of быть. Will be. Завтра будет праздник. Tomorrow will be a holiday.

буду.meaning: Future 1st person singular of быть. Will be. Я буду врачом. I will be a doctor.

будешь.meaning: Future 2nd person singular of быть. Will be (you). Ты будешь счастлив. You will be happy.

будут.meaning: Future 3rd person plural of быть. Will be (they). Они будут дома. They will be home.

могу.meaning: Present 1st person singular of мочь. Can/may. Я могу помочь. I can help.

можешь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular of мочь. Can you. Ты можешь пойти? Can you go?

может.meaning: Present 3rd person singular of мочь. Can/may. Он может приехать. He can arrive.

можем.meaning: Present 1st person plural of мочь. Can we. Мы можем работать. We can work.

можете.meaning: Present 2nd person plural of мочь. Can you (plural/formal). Вы можете остаться. You can stay.

могут.meaning: Present 3rd person plural of мочь. Can they. Они могут прийти. They can come.

мочь.meaning: Infinitive of мочь. Can/may/be able to. Я не могу это сделать. I cannot do this.

хочу.meaning: Present 1st person singular of хотеть. Want. Я хочу пить. I want to drink.

хочешь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular of хотеть. Want you. Ты хочешь остаться? Do you want to stay?

хочет.meaning: Present 3rd person singular of хотеть. Wants. Он хочет спать. He wants to sleep.

хотим.meaning: Present 1st person plural of хотеть. Want we. Мы хотим учиться. We want to study.

хотите.meaning: Present 2nd person plural of хотеть. Want you (plural/formal). Вы хотите кофе? Do you want coffee?

хотят.meaning: Present 3rd person plural of хотеть. Want they. Они хотят отдыхать. They want to rest.

хотеть.meaning: Infinitive of хотеть. To want. Я не хочу уходить. I don't want to leave.

должен.meaning: Must/obliged (masculine past short-form adjective). Я должен работать. I must work.

должна.meaning: Must (feminine past short-form adjective). Она должна прийти. She must come.

должно.meaning: Must (neuter past short-form adjective, impersonal). Это должно быть сделано. This must be done.

должны.meaning: Must (plural past short-form adjective). Они должны учиться. They must study.

нужно.meaning: Need/necessary (impersonal neuter predicate). Мне нужна помощь. I need help.

можно.meaning: Can/allowed/possible (impersonal neuter predicate). Здесь можно курить. One can smoke here.

нельзя.meaning: Cannot/not allowed (impersonal neuter predicate, negative). Здесь нельзя шумить. One cannot make noise here.

читать.meaning: Infinitive. To read (imperfective). Я люблю читать книги. I love to read books.

читаю.meaning: Present 1st person singular. Read/am reading (imperfective). Я читаю книгу. I am reading a book.

читаешь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. Read (imperfective, you). Ты читаешь газету? Are you reading the newspaper?

читает.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Reads (imperfective). Она читает роман. She is reading a novel.

читаем.meaning: Present 1st person plural. Read (imperfective, we). Мы читаем вместе. We read together.

читаете.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. Read (imperfective, you formal). Вы читаете письмо. You are reading the letter.

читают.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. Read (imperfective, they). Они читают книги. They are reading books.

читал.meaning: Past masculine singular. Read/was reading (imperfective). Он читал весь день. He was reading all day.

читала.meaning: Past feminine singular. Read (imperfective, she was reading). Она читала газету. She was reading the newspaper.

читало.meaning: Past neuter singular. Read (imperfective, it was reading). Это читало странно. It read strangely (rare).

читали.meaning: Past plural. Read (imperfective, they were reading). Они читали быстро. They were reading quickly.

прочитать.meaning: Infinitive. To read (perfective). Я прочитал эту книгу вчера. I read that book yesterday.

прочитал.meaning: Past masculine singular. Read/finished reading (perfective). Он прочитал книгу. He read the book.

прочитала.meaning: Past feminine singular. Read (perfective, she finished reading). Она прочитала письмо. She read the letter.

прочитали.meaning: Past plural. Read (perfective, they finished reading). Они прочитали всё. They read everything.

писать.meaning: Infinitive. To write (imperfective). Я люблю писать письма. I love to write letters.

пишу.meaning: Present 1st person singular. Write/am writing (imperfective). Я пишу стихи. I am writing poetry.

пишешь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. Write (imperfective, you). Ты пишешь доклад? Are you writing a report?

пишет.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Writes (imperfective). Он пишет роман. He is writing a novel.

пишем.meaning: Present 1st person plural. Write (imperfective, we). Мы пишем тесты. We are writing tests.

пишете.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. Write (imperfective, you formal). Вы пишете книгу? Are you writing a book?

пишут.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. Write (imperfective, they). Они пишут быстро. They write quickly.

писал.meaning: Past masculine singular. Wrote/was writing (imperfective). Он писал письмо. He was writing a letter.

писала.meaning: Past feminine singular. Wrote (imperfective, she was writing). Она писала стихи. She was writing poetry.

писали.meaning: Past plural. Wrote (imperfective, they were writing). Они писали доклады. They were writing reports.

написать.meaning: Infinitive. To write (perfective). Я написал письмо вчера. I wrote a letter yesterday.

написал.meaning: Past masculine singular. Wrote/finished writing (perfective). Он написал книгу. He wrote the book.

написала.meaning: Past feminine singular. Wrote (perfective, she finished). Она написала письмо. She wrote the letter.

написали.meaning: Past plural. Wrote (perfective, they finished). Они написали всё. They wrote everything.

говорить.meaning: Infinitive. To say/speak (imperfective). Он часто говорит. He often speaks.

говорю.meaning: Present 1st person singular. Say/speak (imperfective). Я говорю по-русски. I speak Russian.

говоришь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. Say/speak (imperfective, you). Ты говоришь тихо. You speak quietly.

говорит.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Says/speaks (imperfective). Она говорит правду. She speaks the truth.

говорим.meaning: Present 1st person plural. Say/speak (imperfective, we). Мы говорим по телефону. We're talking on the phone.

говорите.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. Say/speak (imperfective, you formal). Вы говорите медленно. You speak slowly.

говорят.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. Say/speak (imperfective, they). Они говорят громко. They speak loudly.

сказать.meaning: Infinitive. To say (perfective). Я хочу сказать правду. I want to say the truth.

сказал.meaning: Past masculine singular. Said (perfective). Он сказал правду. He said the truth.

сказала.meaning: Past feminine singular. Said (perfective, she). Она сказала нет. She said no.

сказали.meaning: Past plural. Said (perfective, they). Они сказали спасибо. They said thank you.

делать.meaning: Infinitive. To do/make (imperfective). Я делаю работу. I am doing work.

делаю.meaning: Present 1st person singular. Do/make (imperfective). Я делаю уроки. I am doing my homework.

делаешь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. Do/make (imperfective, you). Ты делаешь ошибки? Do you make mistakes?

делает.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Does/makes (imperfective). Он делает карьеру. He is making a career.

делаем.meaning: Present 1st person plural. Do/make (imperfective, we). Мы делаем проект. We are doing a project.

делаете.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. Do/make (imperfective, you formal). Вы делаете добро. You are doing good.

делают.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. Do/make (imperfective, they). Они делают ремонт. They are doing repairs.

сделать.meaning: Infinitive. To do/make (perfective). Я сделаю это завтра. I will do this tomorrow.

сделал.meaning: Past masculine singular. Did/made (perfective). Он сделал ошибку. He made a mistake.

сделала.meaning: Past feminine singular. Did/made (perfective, she). Она сделала открытие. She made a discovery.

сделали.meaning: Past plural. Did/made (perfective, they). Они сделали отличную работу. They did excellent work.

идти.meaning: Infinitive. To go (on foot, determinate, imperfective). Я иду в школу. I'm going to school.

иду.meaning: Present 1st person singular. Go (determinate). Я иду быстро. I'm walking quickly.

идёшь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. Go (determinate, you). Ты идёшь куда? Where are you going?

идёт.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Goes (determinate). Она идёт в магазин. She's going to the store.

идём.meaning: Present 1st person plural. Go (determinate, we). Мы идём в кино. We're going to the movies.

идёте.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. Go (determinate, you formal). Вы идёте в театр? Are you going to the theater?

идут.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. Go (determinate, they). Они идут медленно. They're walking slowly.

ходить.meaning: Infinitive. To go/walk (indeterminate, imperfective). Я часто хожу в лес. I often go to the forest.

хожу.meaning: Present 1st person singular. Go/walk (indeterminate). Я хожу в спортзал три раза в неделю. I go to the gym three times a week.

ходишь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. Go/walk (indeterminate, you). Ты ходишь пешком? Do you walk?

ходит.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Goes/walks (indeterminate). Она ходит на работу. She goes to work.

ходим.meaning: Present 1st person plural. Go/walk (indeterminate, we). Мы ходим вместе. We go together.

ходите.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. Go/walk (indeterminate, you formal). Вы ходите в библиотеку? Do you go to the library?

ходят.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. Go/walk (indeterminate, they). Они ходят в школу. They go to school.

видеть.meaning: Infinitive. To see (imperfective). Я вижу дом. I see a house.

вижу.meaning: Present 1st person singular. See (imperfective). Я вижу друга. I see a friend.

видишь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. See (imperfective, you). Ты видишь звёзды? Do you see the stars?

видит.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Sees (imperfective). Он видит меня. He sees me.

видим.meaning: Present 1st person plural. See (imperfective, we). Мы видим радугу. We see a rainbow.

видите.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. See (imperfective, you formal). Вы видите различие? Do you see the difference?

видят.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. See (imperfective, they). Они видят проблему. They see the problem.

любить.meaning: Infinitive. To love (imperfective). Я люблю этот город. I love this city.

люблю.meaning: Present 1st person singular. Love (imperfective). Я люблю читать. I love to read.

любишь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. Love (imperfective, you). Ты любишь кофе? Do you love coffee?

любит.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Loves (imperfective). Она любит музыку. She loves music.

любим.meaning: Present 1st person plural. Love (imperfective, we). Мы любим путешествовать. We love to travel.

любите.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. Love (imperfective, you formal). Вы любите классику? Do you love classics?

любят.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. Love (imperfective, they). Они любят спорт. They love sports.

работать.meaning: Infinitive. To work (imperfective). Я работаю врачом. I work as a doctor.

работаю.meaning: Present 1st person singular. Work (imperfective). Я работаю в больнице. I work in a hospital.

работаешь.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. Work (imperfective, you). Ты работаешь дома? Do you work at home?

работает.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Works (imperfective). Она работает учительницей. She works as a teacher.

работаем.meaning: Present 1st person plural. Work (imperfective, we). Мы работаем вместе. We work together.

работаете.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. Work (imperfective, you formal). Вы работаете полный день? Do you work full-time?

работают.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. Work (imperfective, they). Они работают в офисе. They work in an office.

учиться.meaning: Infinitive. To study/learn (imperfective reflexive). Я учусь в университете. I study at university.

учусь.meaning: Present 1st person singular. Study (imperfective). Я учусь музыке. I'm studying music.

учишься.meaning: Present 2nd person singular. Study (imperfective, you). Ты учишься хорошо? Do you study well?

учится.meaning: Present 3rd person singular. Studies (imperfective). Он учится в школе. He studies at school.

учимся.meaning: Present 1st person plural. Study (imperfective, we). Мы учимся вместе. We study together.

учитесь.meaning: Present 2nd person plural. Study (imperfective, you formal). Вы учитесь ночью? Do you study at night?

учатся.meaning: Present 3rd person plural. Study (imperfective, they). Они учатся в университете. They study at university.

один.meaning: Masculine nominative cardinal numeral. One. У меня один брат. I have one brother.

одна.meaning: Feminine nominative cardinal numeral. One. У меня одна сестра. I have one sister.

одно.meaning: Neuter nominative cardinal numeral. One. Одно яблоко упало. One apple fell.

два.meaning: Masculine nominative cardinal numeral. Two. У меня два карандаша. I have two pencils.

две.meaning: Feminine nominative cardinal numeral. Two. У меня две книги. I have two books.

три.meaning: Nominative cardinal numeral. Three. Я вижу три дома. I see three houses.

четыре.meaning: Nominative cardinal numeral. Four. Ко мне пришли четыре друга. Four friends came to me.

пять.meaning: Nominative cardinal numeral. Five. Я съел пять яблок. I ate five apples.

шесть.meaning: Nominative cardinal numeral. Six. В классе шесть столов. There are six desks in the class.

семь.meaning: Nominative cardinal numeral. Seven. Неделя имеет семь дней. A week has seven days.

восемь.meaning: Nominative cardinal numeral. Eight. Моне восемь сестёр. I have eight sisters.

девять.meaning: Nominative cardinal numeral. Nine. В году девять месяцев? (Not counting one.) The year has twelve months.

десять.meaning: Nominative cardinal numeral. Ten. У нас десять студентов. We have ten students.

первый.meaning: Ordinal masculine nominative. First. Первый день осени. The first day of autumn.

первая.meaning: Ordinal feminine nominative. First. Первая помощь важна. First aid is important.

первое.meaning: Ordinal neuter nominative. First. Первое блюдо - суп. The first course is soup.

первые.meaning: Ordinal plural nominative. First. Первые три года были трудными. The first three years were difficult.

второй.meaning: Ordinal masculine nominative. Second. Второй день недели - вторник. The second day of the week is Tuesday.

вторая.meaning: Ordinal feminine nominative. Second. Вторая попытка. The second attempt.

третий.meaning: Ordinal masculine nominative. Third. Третий раз. The third time.

очень.meaning: Adverb. Very/much. Это очень хорошо. This is very good.

здесь.meaning: Adverb. Here. Он живёт здесь. He lives here.

там.meaning: Adverb. There. Парк находится там. The park is there.

сейчас.meaning: Adverb. Now/right now. Я занят сейчас. I'm busy right now.

теперь.meaning: Adverb. Now. Теперь все понимают. Now everyone understands.

тогда.meaning: Adverb. Then. Тогда было холодно. Then it was cold.

всегда.meaning: Adverb. Always. Я всегда рано встаю. I always wake up early.

никогда.meaning: Adverb. Never. Я никогда не опаздываю. I never arrive late.

часто.meaning: Adverb. Often. Он часто ходит в кино. He often goes to the cinema.

иногда.meaning: Adverb. Sometimes. Иногда я забываю. Sometimes I forget.

уже.meaning: Adverb. Already. Он уже ушёл. He has already left.

ещё.meaning: Adverb. Still/yet/more. Я ещё не готов. I'm not ready yet.

только.meaning: Particle/adverb. Only/just. Только я знаю. Only I know. Я только что пришёл. I just arrived.

именно.meaning: Adverb. Exactly. Это именно то, что нужно. That's exactly what's needed.

конечно.meaning: Adverb. Of course. Конечно, я помогу. Of course, I will help.

может_быть.meaning: Adverb phrase. Maybe/perhaps. Может быть, завтра будет хорошая погода. Maybe tomorrow will be nice weather.

наверное.meaning: Adverb. Probably. Наверное, он опоздает. He'll probably be late.

ли.meaning: Particle (enclitic). Question marker. Ты знаешь ли это? Do you know this?

же.meaning: Particle (enclitic). Emphasis/contrast. Я же сказал! I told you! (emphasis)

вот.meaning: Particle/deictic. Here/behold. Вот дом! Here is the house!

ведь.meaning: Particle. Surely/after all. Ты ведь знаешь? Surely you know?

разве.meaning: Particle. Really/is it? Разве это правда? Really, is this true?

бы.meaning: Conditional particle. Would/if. Я бы помог. I would help. Если бы я знал... If I knew...

красивый.meaning: Masculine nominative adjective. Beautiful. Красивый закат. A beautiful sunset.

красивая.meaning: Feminine nominative adjective. Beautiful. Красивая девушка. A beautiful girl.

красивое.meaning: Neuter nominative adjective. Beautiful. Красивое платье. A beautiful dress.

красивые.meaning: Plural nominative adjective. Beautiful. Красивые цветы. Beautiful flowers.

большой.meaning: Masculine nominative adjective. Big/large. Большой город. A big city.

большая.meaning: Feminine nominative adjective. Big/large. Большая комната. A big room.

большое.meaning: Neuter nominative adjective. Big/large. Большое окно. A big window.

маленький.meaning: Masculine nominative adjective. Small/little. Маленький ребёнок. A small child.

маленькая.meaning: Feminine nominative adjective. Small. Маленькая кошка. A small cat.

маленькое.meaning: Neuter nominative adjective. Small. Маленькое зеркало. A small mirror.

новый.meaning: Masculine nominative adjective. New. Новый автомобиль. A new car.

новая.meaning: Feminine nominative adjective. New. Новая книга. A new book.

новое.meaning: Neuter nominative adjective. New. Новое здание. A new building.

старый.meaning: Masculine nominative adjective. Old. Старый дом. An old house.

старая.meaning: Feminine nominative adjective. Old. Старая школа. An old school.

старое.meaning: Neuter nominative adjective. Old. Старое дерево. An old tree.

хороший.meaning: Masculine nominative adjective. Good. Хороший фильм. A good film.

хорошая.meaning: Feminine nominative adjective. Good. Хорошая погода. Good weather.

хорошее.meaning: Neuter nominative adjective. Good. Хорошее настроение. Good mood.

плохой.meaning: Masculine nominative adjective. Bad. Плохой день. A bad day.

плохая.meaning: Feminine nominative adjective. Bad. Плохая идея. A bad idea.

плохое.meaning: Neuter nominative adjective. Bad. Плохое самочувствие. Bad feeling.

весь.meaning: Masculine nominative. All/whole. Весь мир знает. The whole world knows.

вся.meaning: Feminine nominative. All/whole. Вся семья. The whole family.

всё.meaning: Neuter nominative. All/everything. Всё хорошо. Everything is good.

все.meaning: Plural nominative. All. Все люди. All people.

ужин.meaning: Masculine noun. Dinner/supper. Я готовлю ужин. I'm preparing dinner.

обед.meaning: Masculine noun. Lunch. Давай поедим обед. Let's eat lunch.

завтрак.meaning: Masculine noun. Breakfast. Я ем завтрак. I'm eating breakfast.

хлеб.meaning: Masculine noun. Bread. Дай мне хлеб. Give me bread.

молоко.meaning: Neuter noun. Milk. Стакан молока. A glass of milk.

сыр.meaning: Masculine noun. Cheese. Кусок сыра. A piece of cheese.

яйцо.meaning: Neuter noun. Egg. Два яйца. Two eggs.

мясо.meaning: Neuter noun. Meat. Куриное мясо. Chicken meat.

рыба.meaning: Feminine noun. Fish. Свежая рыба. Fresh fish.

мать.meaning: Feminine noun (irregular plural: матери). Mother. Моя мать врач. My mother is a doctor.

отец.meaning: Masculine noun. Father. Отец работает инженером. Father works as an engineer.

сестра.meaning: Feminine noun. Sister. У меня две сестры. I have two sisters.

брат.meaning: Masculine noun. Brother. Мой брат студент. My brother is a student.

сын.meaning: Masculine noun. Son. У нас есть сын. We have a son.

дочь.meaning: Feminine noun. Daughter. Их дочь красивая. Their daughter is beautiful.

бабушка.meaning: Feminine noun. Grandmother. Бабушка живёт в деревне. Grandmother lives in the village.

дедушка.meaning: Masculine noun. Grandfather. Дедушка рассказывает истории. Grandfather tells stories.

дом.meaning: Masculine noun. House. Мой дом большой. My house is big.

квартира.meaning: Feminine noun. Apartment. У нас хорошая квартира. We have a good apartment.

комната.meaning: Feminine noun. Room. Спальня - тёплая комната. The bedroom is a warm room.

кухня.meaning: Feminine noun. Kitchen. На кухне готовят еду. Food is cooked in the kitchen.

спальня.meaning: Feminine noun. Bedroom. Спальня красивая. The bedroom is beautiful.

кровать.meaning: Feminine noun. Bed. Удобная кровать. A comfortable bed.

стол.meaning: Masculine noun. Table. На столе лежат книги. Books are lying on the table.

стул.meaning: Masculine noun. Chair. Сидеть на стуле. To sit on a chair.

дверь.meaning: Feminine noun. Door. Закрой дверь. Close the door.

окно.meaning: Neuter noun. Window. Окно открыто. The window is open.

школа.meaning: Feminine noun. School. Я учусь в школе. I study in school.

университет.meaning: Masculine noun. University. Он учится в университете. He studies at university.

книга.meaning: Feminine noun. Book. Интересная книга. An interesting book.

газета.meaning: Feminine noun. Newspaper. Читаю газету. I'm reading the newspaper.

журнал.meaning: Masculine noun. Magazine. Новый журнал. A new magazine.

письмо.meaning: Neuter noun. Letter. Получил письмо. I received a letter.

стихи.meaning: Plural noun (no singular commonly used). Poetry/poem. Читаю стихи. I'm reading poetry.

город.meaning: Masculine noun. City. Большой город. A big city.

деревня.meaning: Feminine noun. Village. Деревня около леса. The village is near the forest.

река.meaning: Feminine noun. River. Чистая река. A clean river.

озеро.meaning: Neuter noun. Lake. Красивое озеро. A beautiful lake.

лес.meaning: Masculine noun. Forest. Густой лес. A dense forest.

парк.meaning: Masculine noun. Park. В парке цветы. There are flowers in the park.

море.meaning: Neuter noun. Sea. Синее море. A blue sea.

пляж.meaning: Masculine noun. Beach. Песчаный пляж. A sandy beach.

гора.meaning: Feminine noun. Mountain. Высокая гора. A tall mountain.

путь.meaning: Masculine noun. Path/way. Правильный путь. The right path.

дорога.meaning: Feminine noun. Road. Длинная дорога. A long road.

улица.meaning: Feminine noun. Street. На улице люди. People are on the street.

площадь.meaning: Feminine noun. Square/plaza. На площади памятник. A monument is in the square.

вокзал.meaning: Masculine noun. Station (train/bus). На вокзале много людей. There are many people at the station.

аэропорт.meaning: Masculine noun. Airport. Аэропорт далеко. The airport is far.

машина.meaning: Feminine noun. Car. Красная машина. A red car.

автомобиль.meaning: Masculine noun. Automobile/car. Быстрый автомобиль. A fast car.

велосипед.meaning: Masculine noun. Bicycle. Новый велосипед. A new bicycle.

поезд.meaning: Masculine noun. Train. Быстрый поезд. A fast train.

самолёт.meaning: Masculine noun. Airplane. В самолёте много пассажиров. There are many passengers in the plane.

корабль.meaning: Masculine noun. Ship. Большой корабль. A big ship.

одежда.meaning: Feminine noun. Clothes/clothing. Новая одежда. New clothes.

платье.meaning: Neuter noun. Dress. Красивое платье. A beautiful dress.

рубашка.meaning: Feminine noun. Shirt. Белая рубашка. A white shirt.

брюки.meaning: Plural noun. Trousers/pants. Тёмные брюки. Dark trousers.

туфли.meaning: Plural noun. Shoes. Удобные туфли. Comfortable shoes.

шапка.meaning: Feminine noun. Hat/cap. Тёплая шапка. A warm hat.

шарф.meaning: Masculine noun. Scarf. Длинный шарф. A long scarf.

перчатки.meaning: Plural noun. Gloves. Шерстяные перчатки. Wool gloves.

пальто.meaning: Neuter noun (indeclinable). Coat. Тёплое пальто. A warm coat.

время.meaning: Neuter noun (stress shifts in oblique cases: времени, времени, время, временем, времени). Time. Какое сейчас время? What time is it now?

год.meaning: Masculine noun. Year. Новый год. New Year.

месяц.meaning: Masculine noun. Month. Январь - первый месяц. January is the first month.

неделя.meaning: Feminine noun. Week. На неделе семь дней. There are seven days in a week.

день.meaning: Masculine noun. Day. Прекрасный день. A wonderful day.

ночь.meaning: Feminine noun. Night. Тёмная ночь. A dark night.

утро.meaning: Neuter noun. Morning. Доброе утро! Good morning!

вечер.meaning: Masculine noun. Evening. Хороший вечер. A nice evening.

час.meaning: Masculine noun. Hour. Один час. One hour.

минута.meaning: Feminine noun. Minute. Подождите минуту. Wait a minute.

секунда.meaning: Feminine noun. Second. Одна секунда. One second.

январь.meaning: Masculine noun. January. В январе холодно. It's cold in January.

февраль.meaning: Masculine noun. February. Февраль - короткий месяц. February is a short month.

март.meaning: Masculine noun. March. В марте весна. In March it's spring.

апрель.meaning: Masculine noun. April. Апрель - месяц цветов. April is the month of flowers.

май.meaning: Masculine noun. May. В мае тепло. It's warm in May.

июнь.meaning: Masculine noun. June. Июнь - начало лета. June is the beginning of summer.

июль.meaning: Masculine noun. July. В июле жарко. It's hot in July.

август.meaning: Masculine noun. August. Август - последний месяц лета. August is the last month of summer.

сентябрь.meaning: Masculine noun. September. В сентябре начинается учеба. School starts in September.

октябрь.meaning: Masculine noun. October. Октябрь - золотой месяц. October is the golden month.

ноябрь.meaning: Masculine noun. November. В ноябре холодает. It gets cold in November.

декабрь.meaning: Masculine noun. December. Декабрь - месяц праздников. December is the month of holidays.

понедельник.meaning: Masculine noun. Monday. Понедельник - первый день недели. Monday is the first day of the week.

вторник.meaning: Masculine noun. Tuesday. Вторник - второй день недели. Tuesday is the second day of the week.

среда.meaning: Feminine noun. Wednesday. В среду много дел. There's a lot to do on Wednesday.

четверг.meaning: Masculine noun. Thursday. Четверг - почти конец недели. Thursday is almost the end of the week.

пятница.meaning: Feminine noun. Friday. Пятница - хороший день. Friday is a good day.

суббота.meaning: Feminine noun. Saturday. В субботу я отдыхаю. I rest on Saturday.

воскресенье.meaning: Neuter noun. Sunday. Воскресенье - последний день недели. Sunday is the last day of the week.

работа.meaning: Feminine noun. Work. Трудная работа. Difficult work.

забава.meaning: Feminine noun (archaic/poetic). Amusement/fun. Детская забава. Child's play (archaic).

игра.meaning: Feminine noun. Game. Интересная игра. An interesting game.

спорт.meaning: Masculine noun. Sport. Я люблю спорт. I love sports.

музыка.meaning: Feminine noun. Music. Красивая музыка. Beautiful music.

танец.meaning: Masculine noun. Dance. Грациозный танец. A graceful dance.

песня.meaning: Feminine noun. Song. Красивая песня. A beautiful song.

фильм.meaning: Masculine noun. Film/movie. Интересный фильм. An interesting film.

театр.meaning: Masculine noun. Theater. В театре спектакль. There's a performance in the theater.

кино.meaning: Neuter noun (indeclinable). Cinema/movie. Пойти в кино. To go to the movies.

выставка.meaning: Feminine noun. Exhibition. Современная выставка. A modern exhibition.

музей.meaning: Masculine noun. Museum. Большой музей. A big museum.

галерея.meaning: Feminine noun. Gallery. Художественная галерея. An art gallery.

концерт.meaning: Masculine noun. Concert. Классический концерт. A classical concert.

здоровье.meaning: Neuter noun. Health. Хорошее здоровье. Good health.

болезнь.meaning: Feminine noun. Illness/disease. Серьёзная болезнь. A serious illness.

врач.meaning: Masculine noun. Doctor. Хороший врач. A good doctor.

медсестра.meaning: Feminine noun. Nurse. Добрая медсестра. A kind nurse.

больница.meaning: Feminine noun. Hospital. Современная больница. A modern hospital.

аптека.meaning: Feminine noun. Pharmacy. Возле дома аптека. There's a pharmacy near the house.

лекарство.meaning: Neuter noun. Medicine/medication. Сильное лекарство. A strong medicine.

лицо.meaning: Neuter noun. Face/person. Красивое лицо. A beautiful face.

голова.meaning: Feminine noun. Head. Светлая голова. A bright mind (lit: bright head).

волосы.meaning: Plural noun. Hair. Чёрные волосы. Black hair.

глаз.meaning: Masculine noun. Eye (irregular plural: глаза). Голубые глаза. Blue eyes.

нос.meaning: Masculine noun. Nose. Прямой нос. A straight nose.

рот.meaning: Masculine noun. Mouth. Красивый рот. A beautiful mouth.

зубы.meaning: Plural noun. Teeth. Белые зубы. White teeth.

язык.meaning: Masculine noun. Tongue/language. Русский язык. The Russian language.

ухо.meaning: Neuter noun. Ear. Острое ухо. A keen ear.

рука.meaning: Feminine noun. Hand/arm. Правая рука. The right hand.

пальцы.meaning: Plural noun. Fingers/toes. Длинные пальцы. Long fingers.

нога.meaning: Feminine noun. Leg/foot. Левая нога. The left leg.

спина.meaning: Feminine noun. Back. Прямая спина. A straight back.

живот.meaning: Masculine noun. Stomach/belly. Плоский живот. A flat stomach.

грудь.meaning: Feminine noun. Chest/breast. Широкая грудь. A broad chest.

сердце.meaning: Neuter noun. Heart. Доброе сердце. A kind heart.

душа.meaning: Feminine noun. Soul. Светлая душа. A bright soul.

ум.meaning: Masculine noun. Mind/intelligence. Острый ум. A keen mind.

мысль.meaning: Feminine noun. Thought. Хорошая мысль. A good thought.

чувство.meaning: Neuter noun. Feeling/sensation. Благодарное чувство. A grateful feeling.

любовь.meaning: Feminine noun. Love. Чистая любовь. Pure love.

ненависть.meaning: Feminine noun. Hatred. Сильная ненависть. Strong hatred.

радость.meaning: Feminine noun. Joy. Большая радость. Great joy.

печаль.meaning: Feminine noun. Sadness. Глубокая печаль. Deep sadness.

страх.meaning: Masculine noun. Fear. Иррациональный страх. Irrational fear.

надежда.meaning: Feminine noun. Hope. Последняя надежда. The last hope.

вера.meaning: Feminine noun. Faith/belief. Сильная вера. Strong faith.

сомнение.meaning: Neuter noun. Doubt. Без сомнения. Without doubt.

интерес.meaning: Masculine noun. Interest. Живой интерес. A keen interest.

удивление.meaning: Neuter noun. Surprise. Большое удивление. Great surprise.

возраст.meaning: Masculine noun. Age. Пожилой возраст. Advanced age.

молодость.meaning: Feminine noun. Youth. Ранняя молодость. Early youth.

старость.meaning: Feminine noun. Old age. Счастливая старость. Happy old age.

детство.meaning: Neuter noun. Childhood. Весёлое детство. A happy childhood.

богатство.meaning: Neuter noun. Wealth. Национальное богатство. National wealth.

бедность.meaning: Feminine noun. Poverty. Крайняя бедность. Extreme poverty.

цена.meaning: Feminine noun. Price. Высокая цена. A high price.

стоимость.meaning: Feminine noun. Cost/value. Рыночная стоимость. Market value.

деньги.meaning: Plural noun. Money. Много денег. A lot of money.

рубль.meaning: Masculine noun. Ruble (Russian currency). Сто рублей. One hundred rubles.

товар.meaning: Masculine noun. Goods/merchandise. Качественный товар. Quality goods.

магазин.meaning: Masculine noun. Store/shop. Большой магазин. A big store.

рынок.meaning: Masculine noun. Market. На рынке дешево. It's cheap at the market.

банк.meaning: Masculine noun. Bank. Надёжный банк. A reliable bank.

офис.meaning: Masculine noun. Office. Современный офис. A modern office.

фабрика.meaning: Feminine noun. Factory. Большая фабрика. A big factory.

завод.meaning: Masculine noun. Plant/factory. Автомобильный завод. An automobile plant.

ферма.meaning: Feminine noun. Farm. Животноводческая ферма. A livestock farm.

поле.meaning: Neuter noun. Field. Зелёное поле. A green field.

урожай.meaning: Masculine noun. Harvest. Хороший урожай. A good harvest.

цветок.meaning: Masculine noun. Flower. Красный цветок. A red flower.

дерево.meaning: Neuter noun. Tree. Большое дерево. A big tree.

кустарник.meaning: Masculine noun. Shrub. Дикий кустарник. Wild shrub.

трава.meaning: Feminine noun. Grass. Зелёная трава. Green grass.

кошка.meaning: Feminine noun. Cat. Чёрная кошка. A black cat.

собака.meaning: Feminine noun. Dog. Верная собака. A faithful dog.

лошадь.meaning: Feminine noun. Horse. Быстрая лошадь. A fast horse.

корова.meaning: Feminine noun. Cow. Молодая корова. A young cow.

свинья.meaning: Feminine noun. Pig. Грязная свинья. A dirty pig.

курица.meaning: Feminine noun. Chicken/hen. Яйца курицы. Eggs from a hen.

птица.meaning: Feminine noun. Bird. Певчая птица. A songbird.

орёл.meaning: Masculine noun. Eagle. Гордый орёл. A proud eagle.

ворона.meaning: Feminine noun. Crow. Чёрная ворона. A black crow.

голубь.meaning: Masculine noun. Pigeon/dove. Белый голубь. A white dove.

воробей.meaning: Masculine noun. Sparrow. Маленький воробей. A little sparrow.

рыба.meaning: Feminine noun. Fish. Красивая рыба. A beautiful fish.

гусь.meaning: Masculine noun. Goose. Дикий гусь. A wild goose.

утка.meaning: Feminine noun. Duck. Водоплавающая утка. A water duck.

змея.meaning: Feminine noun. Snake. Ядовитая змея. A poisonous snake.

насекомое.meaning: Neuter noun. Insect. Маленькое насекомое. A small insect.

муха.meaning: Feminine noun. Fly. Обычная муха. An ordinary fly.

пчела.meaning: Feminine noun. Bee. Трудолюбивая пчела. A hardworking bee.

бабочка.meaning: Feminine noun. Butterfly. Красивая бабочка. A beautiful butterfly.

медведь.meaning: Masculine noun. Bear. Бурый медведь. A brown bear.

волк.meaning: Masculine noun. Wolf. Серый волк. A gray wolf.

лиса.meaning: Feminine noun. Fox. Рыжая лиса. A red fox.

заяц.meaning: Masculine noun. Hare/rabbit. Быстрый заяц. A fast hare.

белка.meaning: Feminine noun. Squirrel. Рыжая белка. A red squirrel.

ежик.meaning: Masculine noun (diminutive of ёж). Hedgehog. Колючий ежик. A spiky hedgehog.

лягушка.meaning: Feminine noun. Frog. Зелёная лягушка. A green frog.

крокодил.meaning: Masculine noun. Crocodile. Опасный крокодил. A dangerous crocodile.

лев.meaning: Masculine noun. Lion. Царский лев. A regal lion.

тигр.meaning: Masculine noun. Tiger. Полосатый тигр. A striped tiger.

слон.meaning: Masculine noun. Elephant. Огромный слон. A huge elephant.

обезьяна.meaning: Feminine noun. Monkey. Умная обезьяна. An intelligent monkey.

верблюд.meaning: Masculine noun. Camel. Одногорбый верблюд. A one-humped camel.

жираф.meaning: Masculine noun. Giraffe. Высокий жираф. A tall giraffe.

зебра.meaning: Feminine noun. Zebra. Полосатая зебра. A striped zebra.

олень.meaning: Masculine noun. Deer. Быстрый олень. A fast deer.

лось.meaning: Masculine noun. Moose. Огромный лось. A huge moose.

кабан.meaning: Masculine noun. Boar. Дикий кабан. A wild boar.

ласка.meaning: Feminine noun. Weasel/caress. Хищная ласка. A predatory weasel (or: tender caress in other context).

барсук.meaning: Masculine noun. Badger. Норный барсук. A burrowing badger.

раб.meaning: Masculine noun (archaic/poetic). Slave. Раб судьбы. A slave to fate.

хозяин.meaning: Masculine noun. Master/owner. Хозяин дома. The master of the house.

гость.meaning: Masculine noun. Guest. Долгожданный гость. A long-awaited guest.

сосед.meaning: Masculine noun. Neighbor. Хороший сосед. A good neighbor.

соседка.meaning: Feminine noun. Female neighbor. Милая соседка. A nice neighbor.

друг.meaning: Masculine noun. Friend. Верный друг. A faithful friend.

подруга.meaning: Feminine noun. Female friend. Лучшая подруга. My best girlfriend.

враг.meaning: Masculine noun. Enemy. Смертельный враг. A mortal enemy.

враждебница.meaning: Feminine noun (archaic/rare). Female enemy. Злая враждебница. An evil foe.

партнёр.meaning: Masculine noun. Partner. Деловой партнёр. A business partner.

партнёрша.meaning: Feminine noun (rare). Female partner. Танцевальная партнёрша. A dance partner.

коллега.meaning: Masculine/feminine noun (indeclinable). Colleague. Мой коллега. My colleague.

начальник.meaning: Masculine noun. Chief/boss. Строгий начальник. A strict boss.

начальница.meaning: Feminine noun. Female chief/boss. Добрая начальница. A kind boss.

учитель.meaning: Masculine noun. Teacher. Учитель истории. A history teacher.

учительница.meaning: Feminine noun. Female teacher. Молодая учительница. A young teacher.

профессор.meaning: Masculine noun. Professor. Известный профессор. A famous professor.

студент.meaning: Masculine noun. Student. Примерный студент. A model student.

студентка.meaning: Feminine noun. Female student. Талантливая студентка. A talented female student.

ученик.meaning: Masculine noun. Pupil/disciple. Старший ученик. An older pupil.

ученица.meaning: Feminine noun. Female pupil. Отличная ученица. An excellent female pupil.

инженер.meaning: Masculine noun. Engineer. Опытный инженер. An experienced engineer.

архитектор.meaning: Masculine noun. Architect. Талантливый архитектор. A talented architect.

художник.meaning: Masculine noun. Artist. Известный художник. A famous artist.

писатель.meaning: Masculine noun. Writer. Великий писатель. A great writer.

поэт.meaning: Masculine noun. Poet. Лирический поэт. A lyric poet.

композитор.meaning: Masculine noun. Composer. Классический композитор. A classical composer.

музыкант.meaning: Masculine noun. Musician. Талантливый музыкант. A talented musician.

певец.meaning: Masculine noun. Singer. Голосистый певец. A singer with a strong voice.

певица.meaning: Feminine noun. Female singer. Знаменитая певица. A famous singer.

танцор.meaning: Masculine noun. Dancer. Профессиональный танцор. A professional dancer.

актёр.meaning: Masculine noun. Actor. Знаменитый актёр. A famous actor.

актриса.meaning: Feminine noun. Actress. Красивая актриса. A beautiful actress.

режиссёр.meaning: Masculine noun. Director. Талантливый режиссёр. A talented director.

сценарист.meaning: Masculine noun. Screenwriter/scriptwriter. Опытный сценарист. An experienced screenwriter.

продюсер.meaning: Masculine noun. Producer. Успешный продюсер. A successful producer.

критик.meaning: Masculine noun. Critic. Строгий критик. A strict critic.

журналист.meaning: Masculine noun. Journalist. Расследующий журналист. An investigative journalist.

фотограф.meaning: Masculine noun. Photographer. Талантливый фотограф. A talented photographer.

оператор.meaning: Masculine noun. Cameraman/operator. Опытный оператор. An experienced cameraman.

программист.meaning: Masculine noun. Programmer. Квалифицированный программист. A qualified programmer.

десктопный.meaning: Masculine nominative adjective. Desktop-based (modern). Not found in classical Russian.
